Records.description: Prescriptive-unitarity penta-box integral for two-loop six-gluon planar massless QCD amplitudes: a one-loop pentagon glued to a one-loop box along a shared internal edge, with numerator $n_{pb}=-[[j,b,c,l]][[k,f,g,a]]+1/2[[j,b,c,l,k,f,g,a]]$ (Eq. (eq:Ipb)). Called '$I_{n,jkl}^{pb}$' in the paper. Eight massless propagators, six massless external legs, one integral per $(j,k,l)$ label giving three independent penta-boxes up to dihedral symmetry. Provides the two-loop leading-singularity prefactor $R_{pb}=PT \cdot R^{pb,(2)}$. The penta-box cut is one of the five two-loop contours used in prescriptive unitarity. Reduced via FIRE7/KIRA3 to the Henn:2025xrc double-pentagon UT basis; contributes to both pure-YM and $N_f$-dependent hard functions through weight four. Matches integral I.22 of Bourjaily:2019iqr.
Records.location: Section 7, Eq. (eq:Ipb); Fig. fig:six-points; IBP reduction in Appendix sec: IBP reduction

Records.authors: Bargiela, Piotr, Frellesvig, Hjalte, Marzucca, Robin, Morales, Roger, Seefeld, Florian, Wilhelm, Matthias, Yang, Tong-Zhi
Records.description: $I^{*}_{4,1,3}$ two-loop topology with three internal paths of lengths a=4, b=1, c=3 (only one of v_L/v_R carries external legs); 6-point integral, planar (b=1). Geometry: 1 ISP; max cut ∫dz/√P_2(z); algebraic, dlog (§3.4 Type 1.1)
